translations.admin.subjects.create.info: Before creating a subject, make sure it does not already exist as a common subject.
translations.admin.subjects.search.desc: Find subject by code or name
translations.admin.subjects.search.info: You can search for a subject among the {total} existing subjects

translations.sched.course.assign_group.title: Assign group to lesson
translations.sched.course.assign_teachers.title: Teachers assigned to this lesson
translations.sched.time_unit.info:
This fraction corresponds to a possible subdivision of the usual lesson duration. For example, a lesson with a duration of “60” and a division of “1/2”, can be broken down into lessons lasting 30 minutes, 1h, 1h30, etc. The smaller the fraction, the longer the calculations needed to generate the timetable.

If this explanation sounds strange to you, this means it does not apply to you: leave the default setting “1” unchanged.
translations.sched.timeset.info:
A "division" is a split of a single class into several non-overlapping groups, whose lessons are intended to take place at the same time (if possible).

For example: the "L2 German" and "L2 Spanish" groups of Year 9 A.

Group assignment to lessons can be set under the "Hours distribution" tab.
translations.sched.timeset.creation.info: Select a class, then select groups to add to the partition.
translations.sched.spaceset.info:
An "alignment" is a list of groups who must have their lessons at the same time.

For instance: groups 'L2 German' and 'L2 Spanish' of Year 9 A.

Group assignment to lessons can be set under the "Hours distribution" tab.
translations.sched.distribution.subjects.remaining: Time left to allocate, by subject
translations.sched.distribution.subjects.click_to_cre: Double-click a subject to create a lesson
translations.sched.distribution.cant_add_between_asso: You cannot add a lesson between 2 associated lessons.
translations.sched.distribution.cant_drag_assoc: You cannot move a lesson that is associated with another one. If you want to move it, you must delete the association first.
translations.sched.distribution.cant_resise_timeslot: You cannot edit a lesson already placed on the timetable. To change the lesson duration, you must first remove it from the timetable.
translations.sched.distribution.timetable.drag_to_pla: Drag the lesson onto the calendar to place it
translations.sched.distribution.cant_edit_timeslot: You cannot move a lesson already placed on the timetable.
translations.sched.distribution.cant_drag_timeslot: You cannot move a lesson already placed on the timetable.
translations.sched.distribution.import.title: Import hours distribution
translations.sched.distribution.import.only_available: Lessons including a subject which is unavailable for this class will not be imported.

translations.sched.preview.swap_desc: Clicking on a lesson adds it to the transition zone. You can then restore it, modify it, place it wherever you want, or even delete it.

translations.teacher.wishes.newupdate: The teacher updated their preferences after your approval
translations.teacher.wishes.validate.descr: Approve preferences entered by the teacher and modify them if needed (this local copy will not affect preferences entered by the teacher in their personal space)
translations.classroom.infos.error: Please edit classroom details before adding.
translations.home.message.firsttime: Welcome to Omniscol, the online timetable generation and management software! If you see this message, this is your first login or you have not yet created your first timetable. Follow the steps below to get started with the software. The order shown is the recommended one, but you can go back to a previous step at any time.
translations.home.message.defineusers: First add all users: teachers and students. Adding students is optional. Later on, you can create timetables by specifying only the number of students in each class and group. You can also add users later, and only then assign them to classes and groups.
translations.message.hint.table: Hint: you can add large amounts of data by pasting from Excel (or other spreadsheet software) by clicking the icon.
translations.home.message.definesubjects: Define specific subjects on the curriculum that are not included in the default subject database. This step is optional.
translations.home.message.createtimetable: Create a new timetable. It is recommended to proceed step by step, filling in each tab from left to right. Once finished, click the generation button.
translations.home.message.configuretimetable: A timetable is being configured but has not yet been generated. We advise you to complete the setup step by step by filling in each tab from left to right. Once finished, click the generation button.
translations.home.message.distributetimetable: Once the timetable has been configured and generated, it must be allocated to some or all weeks of the school year. Click the allocation button in the management screen. This screen also allows you to find timetables being configured or already generated.
translations.admin.export.json: All school data can be imported or exported in Json format.
translations.admin.import.error.export: You must carry out an export before importing new data.
translations.admin.sites.courses_hours.info: Specify lesson start and end times for each day
translations.sched.time_period.info: This duration corresponds to the regular duration of a lesson (between two bells, for example), plus interclass duration.
translations.sched.distribution.error_assoc_nb_groups: You must select as many groups as there are lessons.
translations.admin.sites.lunch_pre.info: Does the canteen open before the selected lunch break time (e.g. 11:30am instead of 12pm)? If so, a gap in the timetable during this period will not be considered as problematic (unless a lesson must be scheduled at 12pm, in our example).
translations.admin.sites.lunch_course.info: Is it possible to schedule a lesson during lunch break? If so, you must define the minimal duration (in minutes) of the lunch break. For example, with a lunch break from 12pm to 2pm and a minimal duration of 60 minutes, you can schedule a lesson from 12pm to 1pm, or 1pm to 2pm (or none).
translations.admin.sites.opening_hours.question: Do you want to display school opening hours that exceed lesson hours? You can then use the corresponding time slots to schedule meetings or detentions, for instance. This option does not affect timetable generation.
translations.admin.sites.breaks.infos: Breaks scheduled during each school day.
translations.sched.distribution.must_define_site: You must define first the class site hours.
translations.sched.distribution.lockfixedcourse: Lock lesson so that the generation algorithm cannot move it (default setting when manually scheduling a lesson)
translations.sched.distribution.unlockfixedcourse: Unlock lesson so that the generation algorithm can move it if necessary (this is the default setting when generating a timetable)
translations.admin.parameters.common.info: The following administration settings apply to the entire account.
translations.admin.parameters.timetables.info: These are the default settings when creating a new timetable.
translations.sched.displaymode.info: This setting shows the timetable display mode.
translations.format.name: {first_name} {middle_name} {last_name}
translations.sched.course.assign_classroom.title: Assign a classroom to this lesson
translations.sched.course.assign_classroom.none: No classroom assigned to this lesson
translations.sched.course.assign_teachers.none: No teacher assigned to this lesson
translations.diagnose.class.teachers.info: Total instruction hours for teachers configured and actually allocated for each class.
translations.teacher.virtual.info: This feature allows you to create a virtual teacher to continue editing lessons even if you still do not know who the actual instructor will be, for instance if the teacher has not yet been hired by the school. You can then turn the virtual teacher into a real one.
translations.timetable.teachers.transform.info:
Transfer all of teacher’s lessons to another teacher from the list below. The original teacher will be removed from the list of teachers assigned to the timetable.
Warning: this action is irreversible!
translations.sched.distribution.set-classroom.action: Assign dedicated classroom to lessons that have no specialisation and no configured classroom
translations.classrooms.site-mismatch: This site does not match the site assigned to the class.
translations.classroom.specialisation-mismatch: This lesson requires a classroom with a different specialisation.
translations.classroom.capacity-mismatch: Classroom capacity insufficient for this lesson.
translations.admin.parameters.middlename.info: Are middle names used in your country? If you answer "yes", this setting will appear in the user details. Whether or not the middle name is displayed depends on your chosen language.
translations.admin.parameters.middlename-yes.info: Language currently in use supports middle name display.
translations.admin.parameters.middlename-no.info: Language currently in use doesn't support middle names display.
translations.sched.dynamic_help: Only display days with scheduled lessons
translations.sched.modifications.subject.change: lesson formerly in subject {subject1} and now in {subject2}
translations.sched.modifications.course.removed: deleted lesson in subject {subject} on {day} at {time}
translations.sched.modifications.course.added: added lesson with subject {subject} on {day} at {time}
translations.sched.modifications.none: No changes made to the lessons over this period
translations.sched.putinslotzone: Remove this lesson and place it in the transition zone
translations.sched.distribution.cancel: Cancel changes and move the lesson back to its initial time slot
translations.classroom.wishes.descr: Classroom preferences determine the desired classroom usage.
translations.class.wishes.descr: Class time constraints indicate hours you do not want the class to work.
translations.subject.wishes.descr: Subject preferences declare hours where generation algorithm should/must not put the subject.
translations.error.javascript.catched: A JavaScript error has occurred on this page. This may cause inconsistent page behavior and affect your experience. The Omniscol team has been informed and will address the issue ASAP.
translations.error.forbidden_action: You do not have the required rights
translations.error.max_chars: Value length must not exceed {max} characters
translations.error.teachers.not_school: This teacher is not from your school
translations.error.teachers.not_virtual: This is not a virtual teacher
translations.error.students.not_school: This student is not from your school

translations.error.edt.bad_school: This timetable does not belong to your school

translations.error.edt.changes.conflict.class: Conflict detected: class {class} is already scheduled on {day} from {begin} to {end}.
translations.error.edt.changes.conflict.teacher: Conflict detected: teacher {teacher} already has a lesson on {day} from {begin} to {end}.
translations.error.edt.changes.conflict.classroom: Conflict detected: classroom {classroom} is already occupied on {day} from {begin} to {end}.
translations.error.site.lunch_hours.not_defined: Lunch hours of site {site} are not properly defined

translations.error.subject.family.code_exists: This subject's family code already exists

Server error detected.

The Omniscol team has been informed and will deal with this problem ASAP.
translations.diagnose.problem.timesetnb: Different number of lessons for groups in division
translations.diagnose.problem.spacesetnb: Different number of lessons for groups in alignment
translations.diagnose.problem.spacesetnb.descr: Group {group1} of class {class1} and group {group2} of class {class2}
translations.error.algo.configwishes: Lesson #{course} ({subject}) of class {class} cannot be placed because of the configuration of the different preferences.
translations.diagnose.problem.specialisation: Specialised subject without corresponding specialised classroom
translations.login.password.firsttime: Password entered to be changed by the user on first login
translations.student.assign_class.info: Select a class and a period to assign.
translations.placements.school_year_first: You must first define the current school year
translations.diagnose.teachers.info: Total hours for teachers configured and actually assigned to lessons.
translations.data.delete.question: Do you really want to delete this item?
translations.data.delete.noquestion: No longer prompt before deleting this type of data.
translations.admin.parameters.levels.info: Create years (aka educational stages, levels or forms) here, then drag and drop to sort them.
translations.home.message.params: Please fill in your account settings, including class years.
translations.home.showfirsttime: Your account has been configured and is now functional. You can still display the configuration steps.
translations.classroom.specialisation.info:
Classroom specialisation are free labels of room groups to be used exclusively by associated subjects. E.g. practical work in biology, or a stadium for sport.
Most of the time, a classroom has no specialisation.
translations.class.subjects.pw.info: Pedagogical weight of a subject allows to balance days between easy subjects (low weight) and hard subjects (higher weight). Weight scale is relative. This functionality is optional.
translations.class.subjects.specialisation.info: Specify here if the subject must use a specific room group (e.g. a gymnasium for sport). The most common case is to leave no classroom specialisation.
translations.teacher.classroom.info: You can specify a favorite teacher's classroom, to which the algorithm will give high priority when allocating (except if a room is explicitly allocated to the lesson, or if a specialised room is needed).
translations.error.algo.unknown: The algorithm was not able to generate a timetable, but it encountered no specific error. Please, verify your settings, and if this problem persists, contact us on contact@omniscol.com.
translations.groups.import.title: Import groups from another class
translations.groups.import.from_class.info: Groups for this class will be replaced by those of the selected class.
translations.class.teachers.assign.lesson.info:
List of available teachers.
Filter by name and click to assign a teacher to this lesson.
translations.data.offline.enter: Connection to server failed. You enter offline mode.
translations.data.offline.returning_online: Connection to server succeeded. You're back online!
translations.data.offline.loadfailed: Offline mode support is not available in this browser
translations.data.offline.noupdate: You cannot add, modify or suppress data when in offline mode. Only the consultation of the data is available.
translations.admin.parameters.login.info: Automatic generation of the identifier (login) of a new user follows a pattern. If you decide to use the identification number, it must be filled and unique for each new user.
translations.sched.time_period.theorical.info:
Reference duration to count hours worked for teachers and class subjects, which may be different from the real lesson duration.

For example, a lesson is 50 minutes (+ 5 minute break), but teachers are paid for 60 minutes, so you enter 60 minutes here.
translations.sched.generation.consolidate: Inspection and consolidation of the result
translations.sched.generation.action.consolidate: Consolidate the result inside the current timetable
translations.sched.generation.action.consolidate.new: Save the result in a new timetable
translations.diagnose.problem.alert: Timetable configuration problems are detected. Are you sure you want to attempt a generation?
translations.diagnose.problem.group.subjects: Warning, you are using the same group in alignment or partition with several different subjects. This is strongly unadvised.
translations.sched.group.subjects.warning:
When a group is used in a partition or alignment, it is strongly advised not to use it for another subject.

E.g.: even if the Latin group is composed of the same students as the Spanish group, create one group for Latin and one group for Spanish.
translations.diagnose.problem.group.debug: Only view groups related to this group
translations.admin.schoolyear.alternate_weeks.info:
Click on weeks you want to exclude from alternation.
For example, if you click on week 16 and week 15 is a A week, then week 17 will be a B week.
This is convenient for keeping a regular alternation despite holidays.
translations.diagnose.problem.course_teacherwish: Unavailability of the teacher not respected
translations.diagnose.problem.course_classroomwish: Unavailability of the classroom not respected
translations.diagnose.problem.course_subjectwish: Unavailability of the subject not respected
translations.diagnose.problem.course_classwish: Unavailability of the class not respected
translations.sched.type.info: A timetable can be recurrent every week (weekly timetable), it can have an arbitrary periodicity on several days (cyclic timetable), or it can be totally customised on each date (calendar).
translations.sched.type.cycle.info: Period of recurrence, in a number of days, on which the timetable is configured.
translations.sched.type.calendar.info: Beginning and ending dates on which the timetable is defined.
translations.admin.parameters.wishes_teachers.info: Teachers can enter their availabilities or wishes in their personal spaces. Wishes can be defined on week days, on precise dates (calendar), or the functionality can be deactivated.
translations.admin.parameters.alternate_weeks.info: A lesson can alternate from week to week with one or more other lessons (same day, same period). You can name weeks with numbers (weeks 1/2), letters (weeks A/B), or deactivate this functionality.
translations.sched.config.warning: Modifying this parameter will affect already defined lessons.
translations.sched.modification.warning: This schedule is published. Working on a copy is the recommended method.
translations.sched.activation.none: No timetable has been published yet
translations.diagnose.problem.groupsetsubject: Subject not assigned to a class yet used for a lesson with a group of groups
translations.diagnose.problem.groupsetsubject.descr: Subject {subject} on lesson with group of groups {group}, implied with class {class}
translations.sched.distribution.cant_drag_groupset: You cannot move or modify a lesson with a group of groups.
translations.sched.groupset.creation.info:
Select groups to add to the group of groups.
Define a unique name and code.
translations.sched.groupset.info: A group of groups can regroup several groups from one or several classes, to be assigned to one or several different lessons. Its use is more flexible than alignments. You can modify a group of groups later, and also assign it to different subjects.
translations.admin.parameters.type.info: Type of lesson is an optional custom sub-category for a subject. It allows for instance to distinguish between lectures, practical work or exams.
translations.sched.subjects.delwarning: Deleting this subject will delete all lessons which are using it.
translations.sched.subjects.syncteachers: Do you want to apply the teacher modification on this subject to the lessons already defined with the former default configuration?
translations.admin.parameters.logo.info: You can customise your school logo, instead of the Omniscol logo.
translations.admin.sites.distances.info: Enter the transport times between the different sites. This is considered for example when a teacher must teach between several sites. If sites are virtual (e.g. a middle school and a high school not sharing rooms inside the same building), leave at 0.
translations.diagnose.problem.course_distance_teacher: Journey time between sites for the teacher
translations.diagnose.problem.course_distance_class: Journey time between sites for the class
translations.admin.export.share.user.help: Sharing links allow you to access school resources without authenticating. They are linked to the user account, which must therefore be active. An update of the password, even without change, invalidates all previously generated links. Links are valid until the specified expiration date.
translations.admin.export.share.global.help:
The token sharing system allows access to the API as part of an integration with third-party software (backend to backend link).

Authentication tokens provide read and write access to the school account at an administrator level. They are to be handled with great care!

A token is associated with a generated key that can be revoked. The token must be entered in the “ Authorization” field of HTTPS headers.
translations.diagnose.problem.course_teacheravailable: No teacher available for the subject
Subjects {subject} and {subject2} of class {class}, on {day} at {time} and {time2}
translations.diagnose.problem.course_teacherenough: Not enough teachers available for the subject
translations.result.none.precise_id: Please specify your request with an identifier
translations.sched.distribution.calendar.unpublishabl: This calendar cannot be published on any existing school year.
translations.admin.parameters.studentrestriction: Time span schedule restriction for students
translations.admin.parameters.studentrestriction.info: Number of future weeks (including the current one) the published schedules are visible by students.
translations.error.algo.internal: The algorithm could not generate a valid timetable. This may happen when resources do not match, for example if there are not enough time slots to schedule all classes or teacher lessons.
translations.diagnose.problem.overflowstrong:
Number of lessons clearly exceeding the capacity of the time grid.
It will certainly be impossible to generate a valid timetable.
translations.diagnose.problem.overflowweak:
Number of lessons appearing to exceed the capacity of the time grid.
It will likely be impossible to generate a valid timetable.
translations.diagnose.problem.pressure:
Strong lesson pressure to position.
The limits of the time grid will soon be reached. You may have scheduling problems right now.
translations.table.spreadsheet.help: Import and export data in direct edition or by copy and paste from a spreadsheet such as Excel, Calc, Numbers or Google Sheets. You can rearrange the front columns to match your data. If an identifier appears in the left column, then the entity will be updated. Otherwise, a new entity will be created.
translations.sched.edition.remove_courses.confirmatio: Are you sure you want to permanently delete these lessons?
translations.communication.displaypanel.help: A display panel is a web page to be loaded in a browser in full screen mode, in order to communicate lessons or events to the public of the school on a screen, monitor or tv, in the lobby or in front of a room.
translations.title: Title
translations.admin.student.placement_grouphelp: Please click on a group tag and then on weeks of the school year where you wish to assign or remove it.
translations.admin.student.placement_overlapwarn: Classes are assigned on overlapping time periods!
translations.sched.distribution.period.info2: Unavailable weeks (already assigned to a timetable) are greyed out.
translations.email.subject: Subject
translations.email.sendhiddencopy: Send a hidden copy to email address {email}
translations.admin.members.invite_email.subject: Omniscol on behalf of your school {school}: Invitation to join the school
translations.admin.members.invite_email.message:
Hi {name},

You are invited to join the school {school} on Omniscol. Your login is:
{login}

Please, click on the link below to initialise your password. This link will stay valid for {nb_days}.

{url}

The Omniscol team
translations.login.password.help:
The password is encrypted a first time before sending, then a second time during its storage (hash with random salt). Thus, you can only enter a temporary password to communicate to the user, which they will have to change on their first login.

It is not possible to recover the original password, even temporary. You must therefore copy the temporary password to communicate it to the user.



The encryption operation on the browser takes a little time.
translations.login.password.help2: The next step will allow you to copy the new temporary passwords.
translations.dataconsolidated.help: Dynamically consolidated data from published timetables
translations.diagnose.problem.course_sitewish: Unavailability of the site not respected
translations.sched.incompatibility.desc.week: {subject1} can't be followed by {subject2} on the same week
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.selfincompatibil: Not having the same subject twice in the same day
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.maxhours: Maximum number of lesson hours in a day
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.minhours: Minimum number of lesson hours in a day
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.hoursdistributio: Number of lesson days in the week
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.equaldistributio: Spread the lessons evenly over the week
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.info: The generation algorithm will try as much as possible to meet these advanced optimisation parameters. It is not guaranteed to satisfy all these constraints, but it will do its best to do so.
translations.error.algo.cloud: Error in the generation system, the Omniscol team has been notified.
translations.sched.restore: Automatically restore the session on this browser
translations.classroom.maxclasses.info: A large room can simultaneously accommodate several classes (number to be specified), for different courses, with different teachers, with no group relationship between them. It could be a stadium with several PE classes, an examination room or a lobby.
translations.sched.timeset.limit: Restrict to one group per partition
translations.admin.parameters.translations.custom: Definition of custom translations in two ways: general replacement rules and redefinitions of the software's translation keys.
translations.admin.parameters.translations.rules.info: Define your custom term replacements here. Term or phrase replacements will be performed in top-down order.
translations.group.free.info: Group that students can freely join. This flexible, dynamic, and open group does not create conflicts with the rest of the class.
translations.algo.no_support: This parameter is not handled by the automatic generation algorithm.

translations.snapshots.restore.advice: Before restoring, we recommend creating a backup point!
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.dates: Limit generation to a date range (lessons already scheduled outside this range will be ignored)

translations.sched.consecutivedaysmin: Minimum number of desired consecutive teaching days
translations.sched.consecutivedaysmax: Maximum number of desired consecutive teaching days
translations.oauth2.management.description: Manage OAuth2 clients for your school. You can modify basic information, activate/deactivate clients, or delete them permanently.

translations.access.oauth2.scopes: Your account will be used, within the limits of your access rights, for:

translations.staffing.assignment.compatibility: Assignments that can be covered simultaneously by the same staff (symmetrical).

translations.staffing.grids.schedule.warning: Changing the grid will automatically adjust existing assignments for the affected weeks. Do you want to continue?
translations.home.message.defineschoolyear: A school year is a period between start and end dates, including holidays, during which timetables will be published. It is required to access the daily-use modules (timetable viewing, dashboards, absence management, etc.).

translations.diagnose.problem.customevent_classroom.d: Classroom {classroom} conflicts with an event, class {class}
translations.admin.api.mcp:
Connect assistants (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) to Omniscol tools through MCP.
Authenticate either via OAuth2 or using an API token passed in Bearer or in the URL.
translations.sched.generation.tweaks.packeddays: Group teaching days as much as possible
translations.admin.parameters.namecapitalise.info: You can choose to display all or part of people’s names in uppercase letters (last name, first name and middle name if applicable), when the writing system allows it.
translations.admin.parameters.type.mandatory.info: Require assigning a course type (lecture, tutorial, exam, etc.) when assigning a subject to a class.
translations.admin.parameters.loginrestriction.info: Select the profiles that are not allowed to log in to the application.
translations.admin.parameters.subjects.common.info: Disable the display of predefined country subjects. Only custom subjects will be available in the administration and selection lists.
translations.security.sso.info: Configure single sign-on (SSO) using an OpenID Connect provider to allow users to sign in with their institutional account.
translations.error.login.oidc_only: Access to this platform is only possible via SSO authentication.
translations.sched.timeset.unbalanced: Groups in division out of balance

translations.sched.course.status.canceled.descr: Shown as cancelled, ignored during automatic generation
